column1: Title
column2: Authors
column6: Key Contribution
column7: Gap / Limitation

column1: CoTKR: Chain-of-Thought Enhanced Knowledge Rewriting for Complex Knowledge Graph Question Answering
column2: Yike Wu, Yi Huang, Nan Hu, Yuncheng Hua, Guilin Qi, Jiaoyan Chen, Jeff Z. Pan
column6: First to apply CoT-style interleaved reasoning to KG subgraph rewriting; PAQAF alignment improves downstream QA significantly; outperforms prior single-step rewriters on complex multi-hop KGQA benchmarks (WebQSP, CWQ).
column7: Relies on structured KBs (Freebase/Wikidata) with clean schemas; does not address KG construction from raw unstructured text; evaluation is text-only, no multimodal or video setting.

column1: GMeLLo: LLM-Based Multi-Hop Question Answering with Knowledge Graph Integration in Evolving Environments
column2: Ruirui Chen, Weifeng Jiang, Chengwei Qin, Ishaan Singh Rawal, Cheston Tan, Dongkyu Choi, Bo Xiong, Bo Ai
column6: Addresses dynamic/evolving knowledge — important for real-world deployment; converts free-form language to structured KG queries via LLM; outperforms knowledge-editing SOTA on MQuAKE benchmark in high-edit-count scenarios.
column7: Evaluated only on text-based knowledge editing benchmarks; assumes KG can be easily updated with new text facts; does not address video or temporal grounding of facts to specific media segments.

column1: GNN-RAG: Graph Neural Retrieval for Efficient Large Language Model Reasoning on Knowledge Graphs
column2: Costas Mavromatis, George Karypis
column6: Separates graph traversal (GNN) from language generation (LLM), enabling efficient multi-hop path retrieval; 8.9–15.5% F1 gain over LLM-only retrieval on WebQSP and CWQ; works for multi-entity questions.
column7: Requires training a GNN on the specific KG, which is not feasible for dynamically built video KGs; KG must follow Freebase-style schemas; no multimodal or temporal segment retrieval.

column1: SG-RAG: Multi-Hop Question Answering With Large Language Models Through Knowledge Graphs
column2: Ahmmad O. M. Saleh, Gokhan Tur, Yucel Saygin
column6: End-to-end system with clear integration of KG path retrieval + LLM generation; demonstrates multi-hop reasoning without expensive full-KG search; competitive on standard KGQA benchmarks.
column7: KG traversal relies on predefined ontology schemas; brittle when KG paths are incomplete or when questions deviate from KG coverage; limited to text-domain, no video application.

column1: KiRAG: Knowledge-Driven Iterative Retriever for Enhancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation
column2: Jinyuan Fang, Zaiqiao Meng, Craig MacDonald
column6: Does not require a pre-built external KG — constructs triples on-the-fly from documents; iterative gap-filling naturally handles multi-hop questions; 9.4% R@3 and 5.1% F1 improvement over iterative RAG baselines.
column7: Triple extraction quality depends heavily on the LLM used; iterative retrieval adds latency; evaluated on text corpora only — application to video transcripts with temporal grounding is unexplored.

column1: BYOKG-RAG: Multi-Strategy Graph Retrieval for Knowledge Graph Question Answering
column2: Costas Mavromatis, Soji Adeshina, Vassilis N. Ioannidis, Zhen Han, Qi Zhu, Ian Robinson, Bryan Thompson, Huzefa Rangwala, George Karypis
column6: Works over custom/proprietary KGs without schema restrictions; multi-tool graph retrieval generalizes across 5 benchmark KG types; 4.5% point improvement over the second-best graph retrieval method; open-sourced.
column7: Requires a pre-existing, well-connected KG; does not address KG construction from raw video content; OpenCypher query generation assumes a graph database backend not typical in video RAG systems.

column1: M³GQA: A Multi-Entity Multi-Hop Multi-Setting Graph Question Answering Benchmark
column2: Boci Peng, Yongchao Liu, Xiaohe Bo, Jiaxin Guo, Yun Zhu, Xuanbo Fan, Chuntao Hong, Yan Zhang
column6: First benchmark specifically designed to evaluate GraphRAG on multi-entity multi-hop questions; six settings provide comprehensive evaluation coverage; reveals that current GraphRAG methods struggle significantly on complex entity combinations.
column7: Benchmark is text/KG-only; does not cover video or multimodal KG settings; constructed from structured Wikipedia KGs, not domain-specific educational content.

column1: KG2RAG: Knowledge Graph-Guided Retrieval Augmented Generation
column2: Xiangrong Zhu, Yuexiang Xie, Yi Liu, Yaliang Li, Wei Hu
column6: Improves diversity and coherence of retrieved context using KG structure; does not require multi-hop path reasoning — chunk expansion is efficient; modular design compatible with existing RAG systems.
column7: Single-hop: one round of KG-guided expansion, no iterative multi-hop traversal; KG is built from text chunks, not from video segments; no temporal or sequential ordering of content.

column1: GraphRAG-FI: Empowering GraphRAG with Knowledge Filtering and Integration
column2: Kai Guo, Harry Shomer, Shenglai Zeng, Haoyu Han, Yu Wang, Jiliang Tang
column6: Directly tackles the hallucination-vs-over-suppression tradeoff in GraphRAG; filtering improves retrieval precision significantly; logits-based integration is model-agnostic and adds minimal overhead.
column7: Filtering is designed for text-domain entity graphs; no extension to multimodal or video KGs; evaluated on standard text QA benchmarks only — temporal grounding not considered.

column1: KERAG: Knowledge-Enhanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Advanced Question Answering
column2: Yushi Sun, Kai Sun, Yifan Ethan Xu, Xiao Yang, Xin Luna Dong, Nan Tang, Lei Chen
column6: Combines noise filtering with CoT reasoning over KG subgraphs; fine-tuned LLMs reduce hallucination; strong generalization across simple and complex KGQA; large performance margin over GPT-4o.
column7: Requires fine-tuned KGQA models; evaluated on structured KBs (Freebase/Wikidata) only; no support for video or multimodal KGs; KG must be pre-existing and well-structured.

column1: Large Language Models Meet Knowledge Graphs for Question Answering: Synthesis and Opportunities
column2: Chuangtao Ma, Yongrui Chen, Tianxing Wu, Arijit Khan, Haofen Wang
column6: Provides the most up-to-date taxonomy of LLM+KG integration patterns; identifies open research directions relevant to video/multimodal KG-RAG; essential reference for understanding where KG-RAG fits in the broader landscape.
column7: Survey does not cover video-domain KG-RAG; temporal knowledge graphs and educational video content are not addressed; gap between text-domain findings and video applications is not bridged.

column1: VideoRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation over Video Corpus
column2: Soyeong Jeong, Kangsan Kim, Jinheon Baek, Sung Ju Hwang
column5: Non-KG Video RAG — Dense-Retrieval Baseline (included for comparison)
column6: First RAG system that uses LVLMs end-to-end for video retrieval and generation; handles visual content directly without caption pre-processing; outperforms text-only RAG baselines on video QA benchmarks.
column7: Does not build a KG over video content — retrieval is pure dense embedding search; cannot perform multi-hop reasoning linking facts across multiple non-adjacent video segments; not designed for temporally-grounded span prediction.

column1: VideoRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Extreme Long-Context Videos
column2: Xubin Ren, Lingrui Xu, Long Xia, Shuaiqiang Wang, Dawei Yin, Chao Huang
column6: First peer-reviewed KG-RAG system for long-form video including lectures; cross-video KG enables semantic retrieval across multiple videos; dual-channel retrieval combines textual KG and visual embeddings; code and LongerVideos benchmark are public; from HKUDS lab (same group as LightRAG).
column7: Multi-hop is implicit through KG connectivity, not explicit path chaining; evaluation uses open-ended LLM-judge metrics (not VQA with ground-truth answers); no temporal span prediction (no mm:ss citation grounding); educational video treated as general domain — no curriculum-aware KG structure.

column1: AdaVideoRAG: Omni-Contextual Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Efficient Long Video Understanding
column2: Zhucun Xue, Jiangning Zhang, Xurong Xie, Yuxuan Cai, Yong Liu, Xiangtai Li, Dacheng Tao
column6: Adaptive level selection avoids over-retrieval on simple queries; KG built from multimodal video-grounded text (captions + ASR + OCR); evaluated on standard VQA benchmarks with ground-truth answers (VideoMME, MLVU, LongVideoBench); integrates visual and textual modalities at both indexing and retrieval stages.
column7: Graph retrieval is single-pass LightRAG-style — implicit multi-hop only, no iterative path chaining; no temporal span prediction (mm:ss citations); adaptive level selection heuristic may misclassify complex multi-hop questions as simple; not evaluated on educational lecture content.

column1: Vgent: Graph-based Retrieval-Reasoning-Augmented Generation for Long Video Understanding
column2: Xiaoqian Shen, Wenxuan Zhang, Jun Chen, Mohamed Elhoseiny
column6: Clip-entity graph preserves cross-segment semantic dependencies; structured query refinement filters irrelevant clips before generation — addresses the noisy retrieval problem; evaluated on proper VQA with ground-truth answers; NeurIPS Spotlight recognition; processes both visual frames and transcript content.
column7: Verification refines one batch of candidates rather than re-querying for missing facts — not explicit iterative multi-hop; no temporal span prediction; no educational lecture content evaluation; graph construction requires LVLM inference per clip, which is expensive at scale.

column1: VHAKG: A Multi-modal Knowledge Graph Based on Synchronized Multi-view Videos of Daily Activities
column2: Shusaku Egami, Takanori Ugai, Swe Nwe Nwe Htun, Ken Fukuda
column6: Proof-of-concept for video-grounded KGs using semantic triples; rich frame-level annotations link visual events to symbolic KG entities; demonstrates cross-modal retrieval via SPARQL over video KGs.
column7: Constructed from controlled simulation scenarios, not natural lecture/educational video; annotation is semi-manual and does not scale to long unstructured videos; no RAG pipeline is demonstrated — KG is used for benchmarking only.

column1: Query-Driven Multimodal GraphRAG: Dynamic Local Knowledge Graph Construction for Online Reasoning
column2: Chenyang Bu, Guojie Chang, Zihao Chen, CunYuan Dang, Zhize Wu, Yi He, Xindong Wu
column5: Multimodal KG-RAG — Image/Text Domain (Separate Section: Methodologically Transferable)
column6: Query-driven local KG avoids the cost of full global KG construction; multimodal (text + image) graph construction is directly transferable to lecture video chunks; interpretable reasoning paths through the local subgraph.
column7: Designed for image-text documents, not temporal video; subgraph extraction does not encode temporal ordering of content; no evaluation on educational or lecture-type datasets.

column1: MDKAG: Retrieval-Augmented Educational QA Powered by a Multimodal Disciplinary Knowledge Graph
column2: X. Zhao, G. Wang, Y. Lu
column5: Multimodal KG-RAG — Image/Text Domain (Separate Section: Methodologically Transferable)
column6: Directly targets educational domain (textbooks + lecture slides + video) — the closest existing work to the project's setting; multimodal KG covers visual and textual educational content; answer verification loop reduces hallucination.
column7: Video modality is treated as a source of extracted entities only, not for temporal grounding; no multi-hop reasoning across video segments; evaluated on closed-domain Chinese educational content — generalization to English lecture videos is untested.

column1: KA-RAG: Integrating Knowledge Graphs and Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation for an Intelligent Educational Question-Answering Model
column2: F. Gao, S. Xu, W. Hao, T. Lu
column5: Multimodal KG-RAG — Image/Text Domain (Separate Section: Methodologically Transferable)
column6: Agentic retrieval strategy avoids retrieving irrelevant KG facts; KG provides structured curriculum relationships useful for multi-hop curriculum QA; practical architecture for educational deployment.
column7: Educational KG is constructed from structured curriculum data, not from unstructured lecture video transcripts; no temporal segment retrieval; single-hop KG retrieval in practice — multi-hop not evaluated.

column1: LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation
column2: Zirui Guo, Lianghao Xia, Yanhua Yu, Tu Ao, Chao Huang
column5: Single-Hop KG-RAG (Separate Section: KG-Based Pipeline, Single-Hop Reasoning)
column6: Efficient KG-based RAG without multi-hop complexity; dual-level retrieval captures both specific facts and thematic context; incremental update algorithm allows adding new documents without full KG rebuild — useful for growing lecture corpora.
column7: Single retrieval pass — no iterative reasoning for compositional questions; KG construction uses LLM entity extraction which can be noisy for technical lecture content; no temporal encoding of video segment order.

column1: GRAG: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation
column2: Yuntong Hu, Zhihan Lei, Zheng Zhang, Bo Pan, Chen Ling, Liang Zhao
column5: Single-Hop KG-RAG (Separate Section: KG-Based Pipeline, Single-Hop Reasoning)
column6: First paper to jointly encode subgraph topology and text for LLM prompting; strong performance on multi-hop graph reasoning benchmarks despite single retrieval step; modular — subgraph retriever is swappable.
column7: One subgraph retrieval per query — no iterative multi-hop path following; requires pre-existing textual graphs; no video or multimodal support; temporal ordering of nodes is not modeled.
